raw_response:
{"rating": 4.8, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report, but the author’s own framing consistently aligns with right-leaning, pro-life perspectives. Key cues include repeated use of right-coded terminology and pejorative descriptions of abortion-rights initiatives, plus one-sided sourcing without balancing viewpoints. The directional consistency supports a Right rating.

Detailed Analysis:
1) Right-coded terminology by the author: uses “pro-abortion-related ballot measure” instead of neutral alternatives (e.g., “abortion-rights”), signaling a pro-life framing in the author’s own prose.
2) Pejorative phrasing that delegitimizes abortion rights: describes Ohioans as codifying the “supposed right to abortion,” explicitly casting doubt on abortion as a right.
3) Emotive, pro-life-aligned language: characterizes a Montana proposal as one that would have given rights to “babies born alive in botched abortions,” a loaded construction that echoes pro-life rhetoric.
4) Negative militarized framing of the policy tool itself: “Ballot measures are particularly effective as an offensive weapon,” portraying the mechanism in adversarial terms rather than neutrally.
5) One-sided, unsourced attributions in the author’s voice: claims measures are “typically propped up by left-wing organizations… out-of-state dark money groups, and billionaires with eugenicist leanings, oftentimes massively outspending pro-life organizations,” a sweeping, disparaging characterization without countervailing context.
6) Headline framing favors the pro-life perspective: “Pro-Life Groups See ‘Glimmer of Hope’…” centers and validates one side’s narrative in the editor’s/author’s chosen headline.
7) Source selection imbalance: the article platforms only pro-life organizations’ reactions and arguments; no abortion-rights advocates or neutral experts are included to balance the account."}

parsed_output.explanation:
This is a news report, but the author’s own framing consistently aligns with right-leaning, pro-life perspectives. Key cues include repeated use of right-coded terminology and pejorative descriptions of abortion-rights initiatives, plus one-sided sourcing without balancing viewpoints. The directional consistency supports a Right rating.

Detailed Analysis:
1) Right-coded terminology by the author: uses “pro-abortion-related ballot measure” instead of neutral alternatives (e.g., “abortion-rights”), signaling a pro-life framing in the author’s own prose.
2) Pejorative phrasing that delegitimizes abortion rights: describes Ohioans as codifying the “supposed right to abortion,” explicitly casting doubt on abortion as a right.
3) Emotive, pro-life-aligned language: characterizes a Montana proposal as one that would have given rights to “babies born alive in botched abortions,” a loaded construction that echoes pro-life rhetoric.
4) Negative militarized framing of the policy tool itself: “Ballot measures are particularly effective as an offensive weapon,” portraying the mechanism in adversarial terms rather than neutrally.
5) One-sided, unsourced attributions in the author’s voice: claims measures are “typically propped up by left-wing organizations… out-of-state dark money groups, and billionaires with eugenicist leanings, oftentimes massively outspending pro-life organizations,” a sweeping, disparaging characterization without countervailing context.
6) Headline framing favors the pro-life perspective: “Pro-Life Groups See ‘Glimmer of Hope’…” centers and validates one side’s narrative in the editor’s/author’s chosen headline.
7) Source selection imbalance: the article platforms only pro-life organizations’ reactions and arguments; no abortion-rights advocates or neutral experts are included to balance the account.

explanation:
This is a news report, but the author’s own framing consistently aligns with right-leaning, pro-life perspectives. Key cues include repeated use of right-coded terminology and pejorative descriptions of abortion-rights initiatives, plus one-sided sourcing without balancing viewpoints. The directional consistency supports a Right rating.

Detailed Analysis:
1) Right-coded terminology by the author: uses “pro-abortion-related ballot measure” instead of neutral alternatives (e.g., “abortion-rights”), signaling a pro-life framing in the author’s own prose.
2) Pejorative phrasing that delegitimizes abortion rights: describes Ohioans as codifying the “supposed right to abortion,” explicitly casting doubt on abortion as a right.
3) Emotive, pro-life-aligned language: characterizes a Montana proposal as one that would have given rights to “babies born alive in botched abortions,” a loaded construction that echoes pro-life rhetoric.
4) Negative militarized framing of the policy tool itself: “Ballot measures are particularly effective as an offensive weapon,” portraying the mechanism in adversarial terms rather than neutrally.
5) One-sided, unsourced attributions in the author’s voice: claims measures are “typically propped up by left-wing organizations… out-of-state dark money groups, and billionaires with eugenicist leanings, oftentimes massively outspending pro-life organizations,” a sweeping, disparaging characterization without countervailing context.
6) Headline framing favors the pro-life perspective: “Pro-Life Groups See ‘Glimmer of Hope’…” centers and validates one side’s narrative in the editor’s/author’s chosen headline.
